rationale: Travel + Finance needs to feel grounded, trustworthy, and experiential. By avoiding generic fintech 'Tech Blue' and leaning into Deep Forest Greens and Terracotta, the app feels like a premium travel companion rather than a sterile bank. Dark mode shifts to a rich Obsidian to reduce eye strain during late-night travel calculations.

rules: Never use Inter or Roboto for headings.
Numbers in balances MUST use proportional lining features or a monospaced variant if available, keeping the tracking very tight (-0.02em).
Use 'Outfit' for all numeric amounts to give them a modern, geometric punch.
NO PURE BLACK OR WHITE. All grays must be tinted with the base green/brown hue.
Primary color is used sparingly for CTAs (Add Expense button, Settle Up button).
Owed/Owing colors must pass APCA contrast against their respective backgrounds.

spacing_and_layout.border_radius: rounded-2xl for main cards, rounded-full for buttons and pills

grid_system.dashboard: Bento Grid (Control Room style). Tight gaps (gap-4). Every pixel utilized. 'You Owe' and 'You are Owed' take col-span-1 each, 'Net Balance' spans full width above them.
grid_system.trip_list: Vertical stack with gap-4. No masonry for lists.

surfaces.cards: Flat solid surfaces. 1px solid border (border-border). NO drop shadows. Background matches 'surface'.
surfaces.bottom_tab_bar: Crystal Glass variant: backdrop-blur-xl, bg-white/70 (light) or bg-black/60 (dark), 1px top border (border-white/20 or border-white/10). No muddy transparency.

components.navigation: Bottom Tab Nav MUST be an edge-to-edge glass bar. Active state uses primary color icon, inactive uses text_muted.
components.buttons: Primary CTA must be Solid Primary Color with inverted text. rounded-full. Hover/Press state adds a subtle scale down (active:scale-95).
components.inputs: Custom Shadcn-style. rounded-xl. border-border/40. focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/50 focus:border-primary. Background is surface_muted.
components.modals: Slide-up drawers (bottom sheets) for mobile interactions instead of center-screen dialogs. E.g., 'Add Expense' should slide up from the bottom.
components.badges: For categories (Travel, Food, etc.), use muted background with primary text (e.g., bg-surface_muted text-text_main).
components.charts: Recharts or Victory (React Native). Minimalist. Do not use axes lines if possible. For pie charts, use the brand color palette (Primary, Terracotta, Sage, Warning).

motion.page_transitions: Subtle fade + slight Y-axis slide (e.g., enter from bottom +10px).
motion.micro_interactions: Buttons scale down on press. Checkmarks draw themselves. Settle-up action triggers a satisfying scale bounce.

empty_states.description: Minimalist sailboat on water. Use this for the 'No Trips Yet' empty state background with a heavy gradient overlay (to surface color) so text is readable.

instructions_to_main_agent: This is an Expo React Native app. Apply these Tailwind concepts using NativeWind or your chosen styling solution. Map CSS variables to React Native theme objects if necessary.
Implement the Dark Mode toggle logic robustly using React Context or Expo's Appearance API.
For the 'Add Expense' screen, use a large, immersive numpad UI for the amount input rather than a standard tiny text input. It should feel tactile.
The JWT Auth + 4-digit PIN should feel like a premium banking app unlock screen (Archetype 1 style, calm not aggressive).
For the Splitwise logic (who owes whom), ensure the math is rock solid, but visually present it as a clean list of 'To Pay' and 'To Receive' cards.
NEVER use default blue links. Use the Primary color.
Ensure 'Settle Up' is prominently displayed in the Trip detail view.

The UI MUST have a custom, polished look. Do NOT default to basic Material Design or generic iOS components without styling them to match the Archetype 1 guidelines. Build cardless or subtle 1px bordered elements.
Ensure 'data-testid' is present on all intractable inputs, buttons, and list items.
